candidate_rule.label: original
candidate_rule.rule: When a lesion reveals a symmetrical feathered pattern with uniform pigmentation and a central darker area blending smoothly into a lighter periphery, classify as Melanocytic Nevus.
preconditions: The lesion is mostly symmetrical in shape
There is a feathered or speckled pigmentation pattern
The central area is darker but transitions smoothly into a lighter peripheral zone
Overall uniformity in pigmentation across the lesion
candidate_rule.rationale: This rule captures all observed key features of a Melanocytic Nevus and aligns closely with its known dermatological presentation.

precondition_ratings.precondition: The lesion is mostly symmetrical in shape
precondition_ratings.comment: Symmetry is more commonly associated with benign lesions like melanocytic nevi.

precondition_ratings.precondition: There is a feathered or speckled pigmentation pattern
precondition_ratings.comment: Feathering can appear in both nevi and melanomas and needs context like border regularity.

precondition_ratings.precondition: The central area is darker but transitions smoothly into a lighter peripheral zone
precondition_ratings.comment: Smooth color transition is more typical of benign nevi compared to the abrupt changes in melanoma.

precondition_ratings.precondition: Overall uniformity in pigmentation across the lesion
precondition_ratings.comment: Uniform pigmentation is characteristic of benign lesions.

precondition_ratings.precondition: The lesion has a regular border
precondition_ratings.comment: Regular borders are typically associated with benign lesions rather than melanomas.

precondition_ratings.precondition: Diameter of the lesion is less than 6mm
precondition_ratings.comment: Lesions under 6mm are usually benign, but growth trends are also important.

semantic_validation.rationale: Most pre-conditions are reliable indicators of melanocytic nevi as opposed to melanomas. While the feathered or speckled pigmentation pattern can be context dependent, it is accompanied by other strong indicators of benign lesions. The rule is structurally sound and suitable for image validation.

rule.rule: Natural language: When a lesion reveals a symmetrical feathered pattern with uniform pigmentation and a central darker area blending smoothly into a lighter periphery, classify as Melanocytic Nevus.
preconditions: The lesion is mostly symmetrical in shape
There is a feathered or speckled pigmentation pattern
The central area is darker but transitions smoothly into a lighter peripheral zone
Overall uniformity in pigmentation across the lesion
rule.rationale: The symmetry and smooth transition of pigmentation in the lesion suggest a benign nature typical of melanocytic nevi. These features are less commonly seen in melanoma, which often exhibits pronounced asymmetry, abrupt color changes, and irregular borders.
rule.label: pre_completion
most_general
rule.rule: When a lesion is mostly symmetrical in shape, classify as Melanocytic Nevus.
preconditions: The lesion is mostly symmetrical in shape
rule.rationale: Symmetrical shape is a core characteristic of Melanocytic Nevus, commonly observed in true positive cases and less discriminatory in false positives.

rule.label: moderate
rule.rule: When a lesion is mostly symmetrical in shape and has a feathered or speckled pigmentation pattern, classify as Melanocytic Nevus.
preconditions: The lesion is mostly symmetrical in shape
There is a feathered or speckled pigmentation pattern
rule.rationale: Combining symmetry with a feathered pigmentation pattern begins to refine the focus towards Melanocytic Nevus by adding a second aspect of the nevus appearance.

rule.label: original
rule.rule: When a lesion reveals a symmetrical feathered pattern with uniform pigmentation and a central darker area blending smoothly into a lighter periphery, classify as Melanocytic Nevus.
preconditions: The lesion is mostly symmetrical in shape
There is a feathered or speckled pigmentation pattern
The central area is darker but transitions smoothly into a lighter peripheral zone
Overall uniformity in pigmentation across the lesion
rule.rationale: This rule captures all observed key features of a Melanocytic Nevus and aligns closely with its known dermatological presentation.

rule.label: most_specific
rule.rule: When a lesion reveals a symmetrical feathered pattern with uniform pigmentation, a central darker area blending smoothly into a lighter periphery, and is smaller than 6mm in diameter, classify as Melanocytic Nevus.
preconditions: The lesion is mostly symmetrical in shape
There is a feathered or speckled pigmentation pattern
The central area is darker but transitions smoothly into a lighter peripheral zone
Overall uniformity in pigmentation across the lesion
The lesion is smaller than 6mm in diameter
rule.rationale: Adding a size constraint aligns with common criteria distinguishing nevus from melanoma, which tends to be larger, thereby increasing specificity.
